title: Benjamin Practice Set 12 — Mixed Review
disclaimer: Original practice problems inspired by Math Kangaroo format. Not official competition questions.

questions.question_text: What is the value of 48 + 27 - 15?
Which fraction is equal to 0.6?
questions.solution: Wait: 3/5 = 0.6. Among the choices, 3/5 appears as option B, so the correct choice is B.

questions.question_text: A rectangle has length 9 cm and width 4 cm. What is its perimeter?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 120 70' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='15' y='15' width='90' height='40' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><text x='60' y='10' text-anchor='middle' font-size='10' fill='currentColor'>9 cm</text><text x='112' y='38' font-size='10' fill='currentColor'>4 cm</text></svg>
questions.image_description: A rectangle labeled 9 cm by 4 cm.

questions.question_text: Which of the following is a prime number?
questions.solution: 29 has no divisors other than 1 and itself, so it is prime.

questions.question_text: What is 20% of 150?
A clock shows 3:45. How many minutes until 5:10?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 80 80' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><circle cx='40' cy='40' r='32' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='40' y1='40' x2='40' y2='16' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='40' y1='40' x2='20' y2='40' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/></svg>
questions.solution: From 3:45 to 5:10 is 1 hour and 25 minutes = 85 minutes.

questions.question_text: What is the product 6 x 7 x 0 x 9?
A cube has a side length of 3 cm. What is its volume?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 80 80' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><polygon points='20,25 55,25 55,60 20,60' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><polygon points='20,25 30,15 65,15 55,25' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><polygon points='55,25 65,15 65,50 55,60' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/></svg>

questions.question_text: If 2x + 7 = 21, what is x?
What is the next number in the sequence 4, 9, 14, 19, ...?
questions.solution: Each term increases by 5, so the next term is 19 + 5 = 24.

questions.question_text: In a garden the ratio of roses to tulips is 3:4. If there are 28 tulips, how many roses are there?
questions.solution: 4 parts = 28 gives 1 part = 7, so roses = 3 x 7 = 21.

questions.question_text: A right triangle has legs of length 6 and 8. What is the area of the triangle?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 100 80' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><polygon points='15,65 85,65 15,15' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><text x='45' y='75' font-size='10' fill='currentColor'>8</text><text x='5' y='42' font-size='10' fill='currentColor'>6</text></svg>
questions.image_description: A right triangle with legs 6 and 8.

questions.question_text: What is 2/3 + 1/6?
How many whole numbers between 10 and 50 are multiples of 7?
questions.solution: The multiples of 7 between 10 and 50 are 14, 21, 28, 35, 42, 49 — that is 6 numbers.

questions.question_text: A box contains 3 red balls, 5 blue balls, and 4 green balls. What is the probability of drawing a blue ball at random?
An L-shape is formed by removing a 2 by 2 piece from a corner of a 5 by 5 region. What is the area of the L-shape?
questions.image_description: An L-shaped figure formed by removing a smaller region from a larger region.

questions.question_text: A notebook costs $2.50 and a pen costs $1.75. Mia buys 3 notebooks and 2 pens. How much does she spend?
The sum of three consecutive integers is 72. What is the largest of the three?
How many different three-digit numbers can be formed using the digits 4, 5, and 7 exactly once each?
questions.solution: There are 3! = 6 arrangements of three distinct digits.

questions.question_text: A rectangular floor measures 6 m by 4 m. It is covered by tiles 50 cm on each side. How many tiles are needed?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 120 80' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='10' y='15' width='100' height='50' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='60' y1='15' x2='60' y2='65' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1'/><line x1='10' y1='40' x2='110' y2='40' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A rectangular floor 6 m by 4 m.
questions.solution: Along the 6 m side: 12 tiles. Along the 4 m side: 8 tiles. Total = 12 x 8 = 96 tiles.

questions.question_text: The sum of a father's and son's ages is 48. The father is 3 times as old as the son. How old is the son?
questions.solution: Let the son's age be s and father's be 3s. Then 4s = 48, giving s = 12.

questions.question_text: A larger region of side 10 has a smaller piece of side 6 cut from one corner. What is the area of the remaining region?
questions.image_description: A larger region with a smaller corner piece removed.

questions.question_text: What is the greatest common divisor (GCD) of 42 and 63?
A shirt priced at $40 is discounted by 25%, then taxed 8% on the discounted price. What is the final price?
Four friends sit in a row of four seats. Noa is at the left end. Lea is not at either end and Max sits immediately to Lea's right. Kai takes the last remaining seat. Who sits at the right end?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 140 40' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='5' y='10' width='30' height='20' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><rect x='40' y='10' width='30' height='20' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><rect x='75' y='10' width='30' height='20' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><rect x='110' y='10' width='25' height='20' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/></svg>
questions.solution: Noa sits at seat 1. Lea is not at an end so Lea is at seat 2 or 3. Max sits just right of Lea. If Lea is at seat 2, Max is at seat 3 and Kai must take seat 4; then Lea is at an inner seat but we can also test seat 3 for Lea which gives Max at seat 4. The only arrangement where Max is directly right of Lea and Kai fills the other inner seat is Noa, Kai, Lea, Max. So Max sits at the right end.

questions.question_text: On a grid with 3 steps right and 2 steps up from A to B, how many different paths are there if you can only move right or up?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 100 70' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='10' y='10' width='75' height='50' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='35' y1='10' x2='35' y2='60' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1'/><line x1='60' y1='10' x2='60' y2='60' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1'/><line x1='10' y1='35' x2='85' y2='35' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1'/><text x='5' y='65' font-size='10' fill='currentColor'>A</text><text x='85' y='10' font-size='10' fill='currentColor'>B</text></svg>
questions.image_description: A grid with A at the bottom-left and B at the top-right, 3 right steps and 2 up steps.
questions.solution: The number of lattice paths with 3 right and 2 up moves is C(5, 2) = 10.

questions.question_text: A rectangular box has dimensions 4 x 5 x 6. What is its total surface area?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 120 80' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><polygon points='20,25 80,25 80,65 20,65' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><polygon points='20,25 40,10 100,10 80,25' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><polygon points='80,25 100,10 100,50 80,65' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/></svg>

questions.question_text: If 5 workers can finish a job in 12 days, how many days does it take 6 workers to finish the same job, assuming they all work at the same rate?
questions.solution: Total work = 5 x 12 = 60 worker-days. With 6 workers: 60/6 = 10 days.

questions.question_text: What is the remainder when 2^10 is divided by 7?
questions.solution: 2^10 = 1024, and 1024 = 146 x 7 + 2, so the remainder is 2.

questions.question_text: A figure is made of 6 identical equilateral triangles meeting at a single central point, each triangle having side length 4. What is the perimeter of the outer boundary?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 120 110' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><polygon points='60,15 100,38 100,82 60,105 20,82 20,38' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='60' y1='15' x2='60' y2='105' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1'/><line x1='20' y1='38' x2='100' y2='82' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1'/><line x1='20' y1='82' x2='100' y2='38' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Six equilateral triangles arranged around a common central point forming a regular six-sided figure.
questions.solution: The outer boundary consists of 6 sides of length 4, so the perimeter is 6 x 4 = 24.
